steps.title: Read the passage for its main point
steps.content: The text opens by noting that some historians differentiate between well-documented Piast rulers like Otto Bolesławowic and disputed figures like Siemowit. It then explains the reason: Siemowit's record in the Gesta relies on oral tradition, unlike records of later rulers. The whole passage is about why historians treat these two figures differently.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, confirm the right one
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘It favorably compares the reign of Siemowit with the reign of Otto Bolesławowic.’: The text does not favor one ruler over the other — it explains a historiographical distinction, not a favorable comparison. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘It describes a process that was used to authenticate a particular Polish medieval text.’: The text mentions the Gesta as an authentic chronicle but never describes how it was authenticated. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘It presents evidence in support of a particular claim about Otto Bolesławowic.’: Otto is mentioned only as a contrast to Siemowit; no claim about Otto is being argued. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘It explains why historians differ in their treatment of an aspect of Polish history.’: This matches precisely — the passage explains why historians distinguish between Siemowit and later rulers (oral tradition vs. direct chronicle records). ✓
That confirms D is correct.
